questions.text: The source of a magnetic field is:
questions.explanation: Oersted discovered that an electric current (moving charges) produces a magnetic field around it.

questions.text: The SI unit of magnetic field (B) is:
questions.explanation: 1 Tesla = 1 Newton / (Ampere * meter).

questions.text: Magnetic force on a charge 'q' moving with velocity 'v' in magnetic field 'B' is given by:
questions.explanation: Lorentz force: F = qvB sin θ. The direction is perpendicular to both v and B.

questions.text: If a charged particle enters a magnetic field perpendicularly, its path will be:
questions.explanation: The magnetic force acts as a centripetal force, keeping the speed constant but changing direction.

questions.text: A charged particle moves through a magnetic field. Which of the following is NOT changed?
questions.explanation: Since the force is always perpendicular to velocity, the work done is zero, so KE remains constant.

questions.text: The magnetic field due to a long straight current-carrying wire at distance 'r' varies as:
questions.explanation: B = (μ₀ I) / (2π r).

questions.text: Biot-Savart law is the magnetic analog of:
questions.explanation: Both describe fields (electric and magnetic) produced by their respective sources (charge and current element).

questions.text: The magnetic field at the center of a circular current loop of radius 'R' is:
questions.explanation: Derived using Biot-Savart law; the field is perpendicular to the plane of the loop.

questions.text: Ampere's Circuital Law states that the line integral of B around a closed path is:
questions.explanation: ∮ B . dl = μ₀ I_enclosed.

questions.text: The magnetic field inside a long ideal solenoid is:
questions.explanation: The field is uniform and parallel to the axis of the solenoid.

questions.text: The magnetic field at a point on the axis of a solenoid at one end is:
questions.explanation: The field at the ends is half the field at the center.

questions.text: Force between two parallel wires carrying currents in the same direction is:
questions.explanation: Parallel currents attract; anti-parallel currents repel.

questions.text: The definition of one Ampere is based on:
questions.explanation: One Ampere produces 2 x 10^-7 N/m force between two wires 1m apart in vacuum.

questions.text: Torque 'τ' on a current loop of area 'A' in magnetic field 'B' is:
questions.explanation: τ = mB sin θ, where m = NIA is the magnetic dipole moment.

questions.text: A moving coil galvanometer can be converted into an ammeter by connecting a ________ in ________.
questions.explanation: A low resistance (shunt) allows most of the current to bypass the galvanometer.

questions.text: A moving coil galvanometer can be converted into a voltmeter by connecting a ________ in ________.
questions.explanation: A high resistance in series limits current and drops the required voltage.

questions.text: The magnetic dipole moment of a current loop is a ________ quantity directed ________ to the plane.
questions.explanation: Its direction is given by the Right-Hand Thumb rule.

questions.text: Work done by a magnetic field on a moving charge is:
questions.explanation: Force is always perpendicular to displacement (v dt).

questions.text: In a helical path of a charge, the pitch depends on:
questions.explanation: Pitch is the distance moved along the field direction during one revolution.

questions.text: The magnetic field produced by a toroid is ________ in the empty space inside and outside the toroid.
questions.explanation: The field is confined only within the core of the toroid.

questions.text: Lorentz force is the sum of ________ and ________ forces.
questions.explanation: F = q(E + v x B).

questions.text: Current sensitivity of a galvanometer is defined as:
questions.explanation: Deflection per unit current (NBA / k).

questions.text: Voltage sensitivity of a galvanometer is defined as:
questions.explanation: Deflection per unit voltage applied (NBA / kR).

questions.text: If the current in a circular loop is doubled and radius is halved, the magnetic field at the center becomes:
Which rule is used to find the direction of magnetic field due to a straight current?
questions.explanation: Thumb points along current, fingers curl along the magnetic field.

questions.text: Which rule is used to find the direction of force on a moving charge?
questions.explanation: Thumb (Force), Forefinger (Field), Center finger (Current/v).

questions.text: The permeability of free space μ₀ has the value:
questions.explanation: Standard physical constant for magnetism in vacuum.

questions.text: A galvanometer can be made into a voltmeter of range V. To double the range, the series resistance must be:
questions.explanation: Higher V requires higher total R since V = I_g * (G + R).

questions.text: Force on a current-carrying wire of length 'L' in field 'B' is:
questions.explanation: Vector form: F = I (L x B).

questions.text: Cyclotron is a device used to accelerate ________ particles.
questions.explanation: Note: Electrons are not accelerated in cyclotrons because their mass increases relativistically too quickly.
